business_model_summary.draft: Apple designs, manufactures, and markets smartphones, personal computers, tablets, wearables, accessories, and related services.
business_model_summary.excerpt_or_summary: The business description identifies iPhone, Mac, iPad, Wearables/Home/Accessories, and Services as the core offering groups.

revenue_driver_summary.draft: The revenue base is product-led, with iPhone and Services the key mix items for understanding growth and margin direction.
revenue_driver_summary.source_label: Apple FY2024 Form 10-K segment and net sales discussion
revenue_driver_summary.excerpt_or_summary: The fixture identifies iPhone as the largest product family and Services as a major recurring revenue contributor.
revenue_driver_summary.filing_reference: AAPL FY2024 Form 10-K, segment net sales discussion

customer_end_market_summary.draft: Demand is global and consumer-centered, distributed through direct stores, online channels, and third-party cellular, wholesale, and retail partners.
customer_end_market_summary.source_label: Apple FY2024 Form 10-K distribution and markets discussion
customer_end_market_summary.excerpt_or_summary: The fixture describes direct and indirect distribution channels and a broad global consumer and enterprise end market.

business_understanding_risk.draft: The early gate should focus on whether the analysis can verify durable iPhone demand and Services mix rather than treating historical scale alone as a sufficient business-quality claim.
business_understanding_risk.source_label: Apple FY2024 Form 10-K risk and product concentration discussion
business_understanding_risk.excerpt_or_summary: The fixture flags dependence on major product cycles and mix as a business-understanding risk for Senior review.
